text:
Once upon a time in a quiet town called Serenity Hollow, there was a peculiar old bookshop at the edge of town. It was a cozy place, with shelves lined with crumbling pages and windowslooking out at a garden that hardly seemed to grow. The shopkeeper, an old man with spectacles perpetually perched on his nose, would chat with you about everything. Every afternoon, he'd tell stories from those books.

The best one was about a wise old owl who'd listen to every bird song. One particular story involved an old owl who'd heard of a mythical gem that could turn birds back into humans. When the owl decided to find this gem, instead he ended up finding friends in a band of songbirds who'd learned magic.

One day, during a performance at the town square, they found the gem. They couldn't turn back into humans just yet. Every night they'd meet under a big oak tree and share stories.
